title: Discord
description: Ready for DMs and guild channels via the official Discord gateway.

title: Feishu
description: Feishu/Lark is an all-in-one collaboration platform where teams chat, share documents, manage calendars, and get work done together.

title: Google Chat
description: Status: downloadable plugin for DMs + spaces via Google Chat API webhooks (HTTP only).

title: Groups

title: Chat channels
description: OpenClaw can talk to you on any chat app you already use. Each channel connects via the Gateway. Text is supported everywhere; media and reactions vary by channel.

title: IRC
description: Use IRC when you want OpenClaw in classic channels (#room) and direct messages. IRC ships as a bundled plugin, but it is configured in the main config under channels.irc.

title: LINE
description: LINE connects to OpenClaw via the LINE Messaging API. The plugin runs as a webhook receiver on the gateway and uses your channel access token + channel secret for authentication.

description: OpenClaw normalizes shared locations from chat channels into:

title: Matrix
description: Matrix is a downloadable channel plugin for OpenClaw. It uses the official matrix-js-sdk and supports DMs, rooms, threads, media, reactions, polls, location, and E2EE.

title: Matrix migration
description: Upgrade from the previous public matrix plugin to the current implementation.

title: Matrix presentation metadata
description: OpenClaw can attach normalized MessagePresentation metadata to outbound Matrix m.room.message events under com.openclaw.presentation.

title: Mattermost
description: Status: downloadable plugin (bot token + WebSocket events). Channels, groups, and DMs are supported. Mattermost is a self-hostable team messaging platform; see the official site a…

title: Microsoft Teams
description: Status: text + DM attachments are supported; channel/group file sending requires sharePointSiteId + Graph permissions (see Sending files in group chats). Polls are sent via Adapti…

title: Nextcloud Talk
description: Status: bundled plugin (webhook bot). Direct messages, rooms, reactions, and markdown messages are supported.

title: Nostr
description: **Status:** Optional bundled plugin (disabled by default until configured).

title: Pairing
description: "Pairing" is OpenClaw's explicit access approval step. It is used in two places:

description: QQ Bot connects to OpenClaw via the official QQ Bot API (WebSocket gateway). The plugin supports C2C private chat, group @messages, and guild channel messages with rich media (ima…

title: Signal
description: Status: external CLI integration. Gateway talks to signal-cli over HTTP JSON-RPC + SSE.

title: Slack
description: Production-ready for DMs and channels via Slack app integrations. Default mode is Socket Mode; HTTP Request URLs are also supported.

title: Synology Chat
description: Status: bundled plugin direct-message channel using Synology Chat webhooks. The plugin accepts inbound messages from Synology Chat outgoing webhooks and sends replies through a Sy…

title: Telegram
description: Production-ready for bot DMs and groups via grammY. Long polling is the default mode; webhook mode is optional.

title: Tlon
description: Tlon is a decentralized messenger built on Urbit. OpenClaw connects to your Urbit ship and can respond to DMs and group chat messages. Group replies require an @ mention by defaul…

title: Channel troubleshooting
description: Use this page when a channel connects but behavior is wrong.

title: Twitch
description: Twitch chat support via IRC connection. OpenClaw connects as a Twitch user (bot account) to receive and send messages in channels.

title: WeChat
description: OpenClaw connects to WeChat through Tencent's external @tencent-weixin/openclaw-weixin channel plugin.

title: WhatsApp
description: Status: production-ready via WhatsApp Web (Baileys). Gateway owns linked session(s).

title: Yuanbao
description: Tencent Yuanbao is Tencent's AI assistant platform. The OpenClaw channel plugin connects Yuanbao bots to OpenClaw over WebSocket so they can interact with users through direct mes…

title: Zalo
description: Status: experimental. DMs are supported. The Capabilities section below reflects current Marketplace-bot behavior.

title: Zalo personal
description: Status: experimental. This integration automates a **personal Zalo account** via native zca-js inside OpenClaw.

title: Agent runtime
description: OpenClaw runs a **single embedded agent runtime** - one agent process per Gateway, with its own workspace, bootstrap files, and session store. This page covers that runtime contra…

title: Agent loop
description: An agentic loop is the full "real" run of an agent: intake → context assembly → model inference → tool execution → streaming replies → persistence. It's the authoritative path tha…

title: Agent runtimes
description: An **agent runtime** is the component that owns one prepared model loop: it receives the prompt, drives model output, handles native tool calls, and returns the finished turn to O…

title: Agent workspace
description: The workspace is the agent's home. It is the only working directory used for file tools and for workspace context. Keep it private and treat it as memory.

title: Gateway architecture
description: Baileys, Telegram via grammY, Slack, Discord, Signal, iMessage, WebChat).

title: Channel docking
description: Channel docking is call forwarding for one OpenClaw session.

title: Context
description: "Context" is **everything OpenClaw sends to the model for a run**. It is bounded by the model's **context window** (token limit).

title: Context engine
description: A **context engine** controls how OpenClaw builds model context for each run: which messages to include, how to summarize older history, and how to manage context across subagent…

title: Experimental features
description: Experimental features in OpenClaw are **opt-in preview surfaces**. They are behind explicit flags because they still need real-world mileage before they deserve a stable default o…

title: Features
description: <Columns> <Card title="Channels" icon="message-square" href="/channels"> Discord, iMessage, Signal, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp, WebChat, and more with a single Gateway. </Card>

title: Memory overview
description: OpenClaw remembers things by writing **plain Markdown files** in your agent's workspace. The model only "remembers" what gets saved to disk — there is no hidden state.

title: Builtin memory engine
description: The builtin engine is the default memory backend. It stores your memory index in a per-agent SQLite database and needs no extra dependencies to get started.

title: OAuth
description: OpenClaw supports "subscription auth" via OAuth for providers that offer it (notably **OpenAI Codex (ChatGPT OAuth)**). For Anthropic, the practical split is now:

title: QA overview
description: The private QA stack is meant to exercise OpenClaw in a more realistic, channel-shaped way than a single unit test can.

title: Matrix QA
description: The Matrix QA lane runs the bundled @openclaw/matrix plugin against a disposable Tuwunel homeserver in Docker, with temporary driver, SUT, and observer accounts plus seeded rooms.…

title: Session management
description: OpenClaw organizes conversations into **sessions**. Each message is routed to a session based on where it came from -- DMs, group chats, cron jobs, etc.

title: Session pruning
description: Session pruning trims **old tool results** from the context before each LLM call. It reduces context bloat from accumulated tool outputs (exec results, file reads, search results)…

title: Session tools
description: OpenClaw gives agents tools to work across sessions, inspect status, and orchestrate sub-agents.

title: SOUL.md personality guide
description: SOUL.md is where your agent's voice lives.

title: System prompt
description: OpenClaw builds a custom system prompt for every agent run. The prompt is **OpenClaw-owned** and does not use the pi-coding-agent default prompt.

description: OpenClaw 🦞

description: Deploy OpenClaw to production servers with **openclaw-ansible** -- an automated installer with security-first architecture.

description: This guide sets up an Azure Linux VM with the Azure CLI, applies Network Security Group (NSG) hardening, configures Azure Bastion for SSH access, and installs OpenClaw.

description: Run a persistent OpenClaw Gateway on a Hetzner VPS using Docker, with durable state, baked-in binaries, and safe restart behavior.

description: OpenClaw supports three migration paths: importing from another agent system, moving an existing install to a new machine, and upgrading a plugin in place.

description: Retry per HTTP request, not per multi-step flow. Preserve ordering by retrying only the current step. Avoid duplicating non-idempotent operations.

title: Web search
description: The web_search tool searches the web using your configured provider and returns results. Results are cached by query for 15 minutes (configurable).

title: Web fetch
description: The web_fetch tool does a plain HTTP GET and extracts readable content (HTML to markdown or text). It does **not** execute JavaScript.

title: Gemini search
description: OpenClaw supports Gemini models with built-in Google Search grounding, which returns AI-synthesized answers backed by live Google Search results with citations.

title: Grok search
description: OpenClaw supports Grok as a web_search provider, using xAI web-grounded responses to produce AI-synthesized answers backed by live search results with citations.

title: Kimi search
description: OpenClaw supports Kimi as a web_search provider, using Moonshot web search to produce AI-synthesized answers with citations.

title: MiniMax search
description: OpenClaw supports MiniMax as a web_search provider through the MiniMax Token Plan search API. It returns structured search results with titles, URLs, snippets, and related queries.

title: Multi-agent sandbox and tools
description: Each agent in a multi-agent setup can override the global sandbox and tool policy. This page covers per-agent configuration, precedence rules, and examples.

title: Ollama web search
description: OpenClaw supports **Ollama Web Search** as a bundled web_search provider. It uses Ollama's web-search API and returns structured results with titles, URLs, and snippets.

title: Perplexity search
description: OpenClaw supports Perplexity Search API as a web_search provider. It returns structured results with title, url, and snippet fields.

title: SearXNG search

title: Steer
description: /steer sends guidance to an already-active run. It is for "adjust this run while it is still working" moments, not for starting a new turn.
